name: Villaviciosa to the Medieval Bridge Amandi and Path El Garrapiellu — circular
name_original: Villaviciosa al Puente Medieval Amandi y Sendero El Garrapiellu — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Villaviciosa offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal beauty with lush inland scenery. The region features the well-preserved Villaviciosa Estuary, extensive apple orchards, and numerous river routes. Runners can find varied terrain, from flat paths along the estuary to more challenging routes through rural areas and along the coastline. This combination provides a range of running experiences for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Villaviciosa The most popular jogging route is Gijón East Coast Coastal Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers sea views along the varied coastal terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Villaviciosa to the Medieval Bridge Amandi and Path El Garrapiellu — circular, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.4 km) path. This route winds through rural paths and past historical features. Local runners also love the Villaviciosa – Town Hall Square loop from Villaviciosa, a 9.3 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Jogging around Villaviciosa is defined by coastal paths, estuary shores, and apple orchards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes with elevation changes. The routes in Villaviciosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 190 runners have used komoot to explore Villaviciosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Villaviciosa?
A: Villaviciosa offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 19 different options available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from moderate jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Villaviciosa?
A: The region boasts a diverse landscape for runners. You'll find flat paths along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa Estuary&lt;/a&gt;, peaceful rural paths winding through apple orchards, and varied coastal terrain with sea views. Some routes also follow riverbanks, offering shaded and scenic experiences.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or easy jogs?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa provides options for all fitness levels. While many routes are moderate, you can find easier paths, particularly along the estuary or river routes like the Senda del Río Ñora, which are generally flat and scenic. For a relatively easy, shorter loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31829812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Square – Villaviciosa loop from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What about more challenging running options in Villaviciosa?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Villaviciosa has routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa – Town Hall Square loop from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.3-mile trail with considerable ascent, requiring good physical condition and suitable footwear.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Villaviciosa?
A: The running routes in Villaviciosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the region's varied terrain, scenic beauty, and the tranquility found along its paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Villaviciosa?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Villaviciosa are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056094558/villaviciosa-to-the-medieval-bridge-amandi-and-path-el-garrapiellu-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa to the Medieval Bridge Amandi and Path El Garrapiellu — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 6.5-mile loop through rural paths and historical sites.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic views or coastal paths?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa is renowned for its scenic beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056087969/gijon-east-coast-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gijón East Coast Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning sea views along its varied terrain. The Villaviciosa Estuary also provides beautiful vistas, especially near its mouth, between the beaches of El Puntal and Rodiles.

Q: Are there running trails where I can see waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area around Villaviciosa features several beautiful waterfalls. You can explore trails that lead to natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1821428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molín de la Peña and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mexona Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5029162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, often found along shaded river routes.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by historical or natural landmarks?
A: Many routes in Villaviciosa offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056094558/villaviciosa-to-the-medieval-bridge-amandi-and-path-el-garrapiellu-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa to the Medieval Bridge Amandi and Path El Garrapiellu — circular&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical features. The Ruta de los Molinos del Río Profundu takes you past ancient water mills, and sections of the Camino del Norte pilgrim route also offer unique historical context.

Q: What are some unique natural features I can explore while running?
A: Villaviciosa is home to the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa Estuary&lt;/a&gt;, a Partial Nature Reserve ideal for birdwatching. You can also run through extensive apple orchards, characteristic of the region, and along the picturesque coastal paths and riverbanks.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Villaviciosa?
A: Villaviciosa offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. The spring and autumn months are particularly enjoyable, with mild temperatures and lush scenery. Summer provides warmer weather, perfect for coastal runs with refreshing sea breezes, while some inland trails might be muddy after rain in wetter seasons.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer a chance to see wildlife?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaviciosa Estuary&lt;/a&gt; is a natural sanctuary known for its diverse species and is an excellent spot for birdwatching while you run. The tranquil rural paths and river routes also offer opportunities to observe local flora and fauna.
tips: Known for its rich history, beautiful old town and medieval architecture. The council of Villaviciosa is also known for its gastronomy, especially for its cider houses and its production of Asturian cider.

Beautiful hike on the Camino del Norte. The paths are each marked with yellow arrows or on the ground with the typical Camino shell. There are various hostels for pilgrims or possibly hotels. I chose Hotel. Overnight in Villaviciosa at Hotel Casa Espana. Hotel recommended, right on the Way of St. James. Villaviciosa is a very nice town with everything you need.
Nice bridge, also known as 'Puente romano de la Parra'.

waypoints.name: Amandi Medieval Bridge (Roman Bridge of La Parra)
